refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analysis of terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to split these into separate sub-FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This avoids conflating multiple requirements into one and maintains solution neutrality by focusing on what the system must do, not how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing elevation and surface features
Create a semantic map identifying and classifying key site features relevant to planning and safety
Integrate terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution modules
Provide an interface for updating and validating the worksite model based on operational feedback
selected_dp.selected_dp: Integrate terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution modules

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates terrain representation and feature classification but integrates them into a single model to meet the FR. This supports independence from specific sensing or mapping technologies and allows modular updates.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain and feature data from upstream processes or sensors
Risk that integration complexity may increase if terrain and feature data formats are incompatible
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the unified model without additional transformation
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating terrain representation and feature classification into distinct DPs if future requirements demand independent updates or different data sources, to further reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the integration and processing of environmental data, including terrain and feature data, to create a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution modules.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive raw terrain and feature data and send processed unified worksite model data to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Provides the unified worksite model data to planning and execution modules for task planning and execution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution is responsible for environmental data processing; low diffusion since most data integration is internal.
Low containment as Vehicle focuses on mobility and control; integrating terrain data here would cause high diffusion to other modules.
Very low containment; teleoperation is for remote control, not data integration; would cause significant diffusion.
Moderate containment if safety uses terrain data, but primary function is safety monitoring; diffusion would be moderate to high.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP because it specializes in environmental data processing and can encapsulate the integration of terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model with minimal diffusion to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-critical terrain data is needed, requiring clear interface definitions.
Risk of diffusion if planning and execution modules require frequent updates, necessitating efficient interface design.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically satisfies the FR by specifying the integration of terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution modules, directly addressing the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-critical terrain data is needed, which may cause diffusion if interfaces are not clearly defined.
Risk of diffusion if planning and execution modules require frequent updates from the NGHE Solution, necessitating efficient interface design to avoid scattered responsibilities.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion and appropriate encapsulation of the DP. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module provides the unified worksite model to planning and execution modules via defined interfaces (Telemetry and Tasking), enabling validation against acceptance criteria such as availability of the worksite model and inclusion of agreed site features.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate (NGHE Solution) is clearly defined with a scope that encompasses the DP, ensuring clear allocation of responsibility for the FR realization.

recommended_revisions: Define clear interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Safety Services to manage any overlap in terrain data usage and avoid diffusion.
Ensure interface design between NGHE Solution and planning/execution modules supports efficient updates to minimize diffusion risk.
Document and monitor the frequency and nature of data exchanges to maintain containment and reduce diffusion over time.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately satisfied by the design parameter and implementation module. The NGHE Solution module shows strong containment and low diffusion, with clear ownership and feasible verification. Although there are identified diffusion risks related to interface overlaps and update frequencies, these are manageable through recommended revisions and do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and well-contained module boundary is preferable to avoid complexity and potential diffusion.

revise.why: To define clear interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Safety Services, and to ensure efficient interface design with planning and execution modules to manage diffusion risks.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can encapsulate the DP effectively without further subdivision.
Interface contracts can be clearly defined and maintained to manage diffusion risks.
Verification methods are sufficient to validate the unified worksite model and its interfaces.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-critical terrain data is needed.
Risk of diffusion due to frequent updates required by planning and execution modules.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts and data exchange protocols.
Monitoring data on frequency and nature of data exchanges to ensure containment is maintained.
